papers.abstract: Abstract Current multimodal models struggle to match human expert aesthetic judgment in comparative image selection tasks, as demonstrated by the Visual Aesthetic Benchmark which reveals significant performance gaps and shows that fine-tuning on expert examples can improve accuracy.  AI-generated summary Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) are now routinely deployed for visual understanding, generation, and curation. A substantial fraction of these applications require an explicit aesthetic judgment. Most existing solutions reduce this judgment to predicting a scalar score for a single image. We first ask whether such scores faithfully capture comparative preference: in a controlled study with eight expert annotators, score-derived rankings align poorly with the same annotators' direct comparisons, while direct ranking yields substantially higher inter-annotator agreement on best- and worst-image labels. Motivated by this finding, we introduce the Visual Aesthetic Benchmark (VAB), which casts aesthetic evaluation as comparative selection over candidate sets with matched subject matter. VAB contains 400 tasks and 1,195 images across fine art, photography, and illustration, with labels derived from the consensus of 10 independent expert judges per task. Evaluating 20 frontier MLLMs and six dedicated visual-quality reward models, we find that the strongest system identifies both the best and the worst image correctly across three random permutations of the candidate order in only 26.5% of tasks, far below the 68.9% achieved by human experts. Fine-tuning a 35B-parameter model on 2,000 expert examples brings its accuracy close to that of a 397B-parameter open-weight model, suggesting that the comparative signal in VAB is transferable. Together, these results expose a clear and measurable gap between current multimodal models and expert aesthetic judgment, and VAB provides the first set-based, expert-grounded testbed on which that gap can be tracked and closed.
affiliations: King Abdulaziz City for Science and Technology

ru.title: Сравнительное суждение об эстетике: преодолевая разрыв между моделями и экспертами
ru.desc: В статье исследуется способность мультимодальных больших языковых моделей выносить эстетические суждения при выборе лучшего изображения из набора кандидатов. Авторы показали, что традиционные подходы, основанные на предсказании скалярной оценки для отдельного изображения, плохо согласуются с прямым сравнением эксперта, и предложили новый бенчмарк Visual Aesthetic Benchmark с 400 задачами на сравнение изображений. Оценка 20 современных мультимодальных моделей выявила значительный разрыв между их производительностью (26.5%) и экспертными суждениями (68.9%), хотя файн-тюнинг на примерах экспертов позволяет существенно улучшить результаты. Исследование демонстрирует, что сигнал о сравнительных предпочтениях в бенчмарке хорошо переносится на другие модели.

en.title: Bridging the Gap in AI Aesthetic Judgment
en.desc: This paper addresses the limitations of current multimodal models in making aesthetic judgments in image selection tasks. It highlights that existing models often predict a single score for images, which does not accurately reflect human preferences. The authors introduce the Visual Aesthetic Benchmark (VAB), a new framework for evaluating aesthetic quality through comparative selection of images, which shows better alignment with expert opinions. The findings reveal a significant performance gap between AI models and human experts, emphasizing the need for improved training methods to enhance aesthetic evaluation capabilities in AI.

zh.title: 缩小多模态模型与专家审美判断的差距
zh.desc: 当前的多模态模型在比较图像选择任务中难以匹配人类专家的审美判断。我们通过视觉审美基准（VAB）展示了这一点，该基准将审美评估视为对候选集的比较选择。研究表明，现有模型在识别最佳和最差图像方面的准确性远低于人类专家。通过对模型进行微调，可以显著提高其准确性，缩小与专家判断之间的差距。

papers.title: ShapeCodeBench: A Renewable Benchmark for Perception-to-Program Reconstruction of Synthetic Shape Scenes
papers.abstract: Abstract ShapeCodeBench presents a synthetic benchmark for perception-to-program reconstruction where models generate executable drawing programs from raster images, evaluated on multiple metrics including exact match and pixel accuracy.  AI-generated summary We introduce ShapeCodeBench, a synthetic benchmark for perception-to-program reconstruction: given a rendered raster image, a model must emit an executable drawing program that a deterministic evaluator re-renders and compares with the target. The v1 DSL has four primitives on a 512 x 512 black-on-white canvas, but every instance is generated from a seeded RNG, so fresh held-out sets can be created to reduce exact-instance contamination. We release a frozen eval_v1 split with 150 samples across easy, medium, and hard tiers, scored by exact match, pixel accuracy, foreground IoU, parse success, and execution success. We evaluate an empty-program floor, a classical computer-vision heuristic, Claude Opus 4.7 at high and max effort, and GPT-5.5 at medium and extra_high reasoning effort. The heuristic is competitive on easy scenes but collapses when overlaps fuse components; the strongest multimodal configuration preserves much of the foreground structure but still misses exact match because of small parameter errors. Best overall exact match remains low, so ShapeCodeBench is far from saturated. The benchmark code, frozen dataset, run artifacts, and paper sources are released to support independent replication and extension.

ru.title: От пикселя к коду: бенчмарк для восстановления программ рисования из изображений
ru.desc: ShapeCodeBench — это синтетический бенчмарк для задачи восстановления программы из изображения, где модели должны генерировать исполняемые программы рисования на основе растровых изображений. Датасет создаётся процедурно с использованием детерминированного генератора случайных чисел, что позволяет создавать новые тестовые наборы и избежать загрязнения данных повторяющимися примерами. Оценка моделей проводится по нескольким метрикам: точное совпадение кода, точность пикселей, IoU переднего плана, успешность парсинга и успешность выполнения программы. Авторы провели комплексную оценку современных LLM-моделей, показав что даже самые мощные модели значительно отстают по метрике exact match, что указывает на высокую сложность задачи и потенциал для развития.

en.title: Transforming Images into Code: The ShapeCodeBench Challenge
en.desc: ShapeCodeBench is a new benchmark designed to evaluate how well models can convert images into executable drawing programs. It uses a specific drawing language with four basic commands to create images on a 512 x 512 canvas. The benchmark includes a variety of difficulty levels and measures performance using metrics like exact match and pixel accuracy. The results show that while some models perform well on simpler tasks, they struggle with more complex images, indicating that the benchmark is still challenging and not fully solved.

zh.title: ShapeCodeBench：感知到程序重建的新基准
zh.desc: ShapeCodeBench 是一个用于感知到程序重建的合成基准，旨在评估模型从光栅图像生成可执行绘图程序的能力。该基准使用多个指标进行评估，包括精确匹配和像素准确度。模型在一个 512 x 512 的黑白画布上生成程序，并通过确定性评估器进行重渲染和比较。尽管当前的最佳精确匹配仍然较低，但该基准为独立复制和扩展提供了代码和数据集。

papers.title: Position: LLM Inference Should Be Evaluated as Energy-to-Token Production
papers.abstract: Abstract LLM inference should be evaluated as energy-to-token production under constraints of compute, power, cooling, and operational efficiency, requiring new metrics beyond traditional accuracy and latency measures.  AI-generated summary LLM inference is still evaluated mainly as a model or software problem: accuracy, latency, throughput, and hardware utilization. This is incomplete. At deployment scale, the relevant output is a quality-conditioned token produced under joint constraints from effective compute, delivered data-center power, cooling capacity, PUE, and utilization.   We argue that the ML community should treat inference as energy-to-token production. We formalize this view with a dimensionally consistent Token Production Function in which token rate is bounded by both compute-per-token and energy-per-token ceilings. Listed API prices vary by over an order of magnitude across providers, but we use price dispersion only as directional motivation, not as causal evidence of marginal cost. The core physical question is instead: under fixed quality and service targets, when does the binding constraint move from theoretical peak compute toward delivered power, cooling, and operational efficiency?   Under this framing, system optimizations -- latent KV-cache compression, sparse or heavily compressed attention, quantization, routing, and difficulty-adaptive reasoning -- are not merely local engineering tricks. They are energy-to-token levers because they reduce FLOPs/token, joules/token, memory traffic, or utilization losses under fixed (q^{*},s^{*}). We therefore call for inference papers and benchmarks to report Joules/token, active binding constraint, PUE-adjusted delivered power, and utilization-adjusted token output alongside accuracy and latency.
affiliations: Guangzhou HKUST Fok Ying Tung Research Institute

ru.title: От вычислений к энергии: новая парадигма оценки инференса больших моделей
ru.desc: Статья переосмысливает эвалуацию инференса больших языковых моделей, предлагая рассматривать её как задачу производства токенов на единицу энергии с учётом физических ограничений центра обработки данных. Авторы формализуют эту идею через функцию производства токенов, которая показывает, что скорость генерации токенов ограничена одновременно вычислительной мощностью на токен и энергопотреблением на токен. Они аргументируют, что различные технические оптимизации (компрессия KV-кеша, разреженное внимание, квантизация) следует рассматривать не как локальные инженерные трюки, а как рычаги для улучшения энергетической эффективности. Авторы призывают переориентировать метрики оценки инференса: наряду с точностью и латентностью необходимо репортировать энергопотребление на токен, активные узкие места системы и откорректированные на PUE показатели утилизации.

en.title: Transforming Inference: From Accuracy to Energy Efficiency
en.desc: This paper proposes a new way to evaluate large language model (LLM) inference by focusing on energy-to-token production rather than just traditional metrics like accuracy and latency. It emphasizes that at scale, the efficiency of producing tokens is constrained by compute resources, power availability, and cooling capacity. The authors introduce a Token Production Function that considers both compute and energy limits, suggesting that optimizations in model architecture should be viewed as ways to improve energy efficiency. They advocate for the ML community to adopt new benchmarks that include energy metrics, which will provide a more comprehensive understanding of LLM performance in real-world applications.

zh.title: 将推理视为能量到令牌的生产
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了在机器学习推理中，应该将其视为在计算、功率、冷却和操作效率的约束下进行的能量到令牌的生产。传统的评估方法主要关注准确性和延迟，但在实际部署中，输出的质量受到多种因素的影响。作者建议使用一个一致的令牌生产函数来描述这种关系，并强调在固定质量和服务目标下，约束条件的转变。最后，论文呼吁在推理研究中报告每个令牌的能量消耗，以及其他相关的性能指标。

papers.title: MemReread: Enhancing Agentic Long-Context Reasoning via Memory-Guided Rereading
papers.abstract: Abstract MemReread addresses long-context reasoning challenges by avoiding intermediate retrieval and employing question decomposition with rereading to recover discarded information, maintaining linear time complexity.  AI-generated summary To tackle long-context reasoning tasks without the quadratic complexity of standard attention mechanisms, approaches based on agent memory have emerged, which typically maintain a dynamically updated memory when linearly processing document chunks. To mitigate the potential loss of latent evidence in this memorize-while-reading paradigm, recent works have integrated retrieval modules that allow agents to recall information previously discarded during memory overwriting. However, retrieval-based recall suffers from both evidence loss during memory formation and interference induced by invalid queries. To overcome these limitations, we propose MemReread. Built upon streaming reading, MemReread circumvents intermediate retrieval. It triggers question decomposition and rereading when the final memory is insufficient, enabling the recovery of indirect facts that were prematurely discarded. This design supports non-linear reasoning while preserving the inherent logical flow of document comprehension. To further enhance practicality, we introduce a reinforcement learning framework that enhances length extrapolation capability while dynamically determining the number of rereading passes based on task complexity, thereby flexibly controlling computational overhead. Extensive experiments demonstrate that MemReread consistently outperforms baseline frameworks on long-context reasoning tasks, while maintaining linear time complexity with respect to context length.

ru.title: Повторное чтение вместо поиска: линейная обработка длинных контекстов
ru.desc: MemReread решает задачу рассуждений на длинных контекстах, избегая промежуточного поиска и используя декомпозицию вопросов с повторным чтением для восстановления отброшенной информации. Метод обрабатывает документ потоком с линейной сложностью, сохраняя динамическую память о прочитанном. Когда финальной памяти недостаточно для ответа, система разбивает вопрос и перечитывает релевантные части, восстанавливая косвенные факты. Добавленный фреймворк усиленного обучения адаптивно определяет количество перечитываний в зависимости от сложности задачи, контролируя вычислительные затраты.

en.title: MemReread: Efficient Long-Context Reasoning through Rereading and Question Decomposition
en.desc: MemReread is a novel approach designed to improve long-context reasoning in machine learning by avoiding the need for intermediate retrieval of information. It uses a technique called question decomposition, which allows the model to reread parts of the text to recover important details that may have been overlooked. This method maintains linear time complexity, making it efficient even as the context length increases. Additionally, a reinforcement learning framework is integrated to adaptively manage rereading based on the complexity of the task, ensuring optimal performance without excessive computational costs.

zh.title: MemReread：高效的长上下文推理新方法
zh.desc: MemReread 是一种新方法，旨在解决长上下文推理的挑战。它通过避免中间检索，采用问题分解和重读的方式来恢复被丢弃的信息，从而保持线性时间复杂度。该方法在处理文档时，能够有效地回收间接事实，支持非线性推理，同时保持文档理解的逻辑流。通过引入强化学习框架，MemReread 动态调整重读次数，灵活控制计算开销，实验证明其在长上下文推理任务中表现优于基线框架。

papers.title: The Extrapolation Cliff in On-Policy Distillation of Near-Deterministic Structured Outputs
papers.abstract: Abstract On-policy distillation with reward extrapolation exhibits a safety threshold beyond which structured output tasks lose format preservation, with empirical validation showing performance parity at reduced parameter count when operating below this threshold.  AI-generated summary On-policy distillation (OPD) is widely used for LLM post-training. When pushed with a reward-extrapolation coefficient lambda > 1, the student can lift past the teacher in domain, but past a threshold lambda* the same step violates the output contract on structured-output tasks. In a single-position Bernoulli reduction, we derive a closed-form base-relative clip-safety threshold lambda*(p,b,c) determined by three measurable quantities: the teacher modal probability, the warm-start mass, and the importance-sampling clip strength. Above lambda*, the extrapolated fixed point exits the clip-safe region, changing training from format-preserving to format-collapsing. We extend the rule to calibrated K-ary listwise JSON tasks where a single binding equivalence class dominates the output contract and SFT retains parse headroom. On Amazon Fashion, three pre-registered tests--a fine-grid cliff interval, a budget-extension test, and a small-clip cross-prediction--fall within their locked prediction windows, with the small-clip value matching the closed-form prediction below grid resolution. Operating just below lambda*, ListOPD brings a 1.7B Qwen3 student to in-domain parity with an 8B-SFT baseline at one-fifth the parameters. The gain is driven primarily by format adherence: NDCG@1 on parsed outputs remains flat across lambda, while parse validity sharply changes at the predicted boundary. The cliff diagnostic is rubric-independent, whereas the parity claim uses a Gemini-graded rubric and inherits that evaluator's exposure.

ru.title: Безопасный порог дистилляции: баланс между производительностью и сохранением формата
ru.desc: Работа изучает дистилляцию моделей с экстраполяцией вознаграждения (on-policy distillation), показывая, что существует критический порог lambda*, за которым модель-ученик начинает нарушать формат структурированного вывода. Авторы математически вывели замкнутую форму этого порога для задач с бинарным выбором и расширили её на многовариантные JSON-задачи. Экспериментально подтверждено, что при работе ниже критического порога небольшая модель (1.7B параметров) достигает качества большой модели (8B параметров), сохраняя корректность формата при уменьшении параметров в пять раз. Ключевой вывод: безопасность дистилляции определяется модальной вероятностью учителя, инициализацией и методом обрезания важности выборки.

en.title: Balancing Performance and Format in On-Policy Distillation
en.desc: This paper discusses on-policy distillation (OPD) in machine learning, particularly focusing on how reward extrapolation affects structured output tasks. It identifies a safety threshold, lambda*, beyond which the performance of the student model deteriorates, leading to format collapse instead of preservation. The authors derive a closed-form expression for lambda* based on key factors like teacher modal probability and importance-sampling clip strength. Empirical results show that operating just below this threshold allows a smaller student model to achieve performance parity with a larger teacher model while maintaining output format integrity.

zh.title: 在线策略蒸馏的安全阈值与格式保持
zh.desc: 本文探讨了在奖励外推下的在线策略蒸馏（OPD）如何影响结构化输出任务的格式保持。当外推系数超过某个阈值时，学生模型的输出可能会违反格式约定，导致训练效果下降。我们推导出一个闭合形式的安全阈值，该阈值由教师模型的模态概率、热启动质量和重要性采样剪切强度等三个可测量量决定。在亚马逊时尚数据集上进行的实验表明，接近安全阈值的操作可以使较小参数的学生模型在特定任务上与大型教师模型的表现相当。

papers.title: F-GRPO: Factorized Group-Relative Policy Optimization for Unified Candidate Generation and Ranking
papers.abstract: Abstract A unified framework combines candidate generation and ranking in a single autoregressive model using factorized group-relative policy optimization to address credit assignment challenges in end-to-end retrieval optimization.  AI-generated summary Traditional retrieval pipelines optimize utility through stages of candidate retrieval and reranking, where ranking operates over a predefined candidate set. Large Language Models (LLMs) broaden this into a generative process: given a candidate pool, an LLM can generate a subset and order it within a single autoregressive pass. However, this flexibility introduces a new optimization challenge: the model must search a combinatorial output space while receiving utility feedback only after the full ranked list is generated. Because this feedback is defined over the completed sequence, it cannot distinguish whether a poor result arises from failing to generate a relevant subset or from failing to rank that subset correctly. This credit assignment gap makes end-to-end optimization unstable and sample-inefficient. Existing systems often address this by separating candidate generation from ranking. However, such decoupling remains misaligned with downstream utility because ranking is limited by the candidate set it receives. To bridge this gap, we propose a unified framework that performs both within a single autoregressive rollout and optimizes them end-to-end via factorized group-relative policy optimization (F-GRPO). Our framework factorizes the policy into candidate generation and ranking while sharing a single LLM backbone, and jointly trains them with an order-invariant coverage reward and a position-aware utility reward. To address the resulting phase-specific credit assignment problem, we use separate group-relative advantages for generation and ranking within a two-phase sequence-level objective. Across sequential recommendation and multi-hop question answering benchmarks, F-GRPO improves top-ranked performance over GRPO and decoupled baselines, outperforms supervised alternatives, and remains competitive with strong zero-shot rerankers, with no architectural changes at inference time.

ru.title: Единая оптимизация генерации и ранжирования через факторизованную политику
ru.desc: В статье предложена единая архитектура, которая объединяет генерацию кандидатов и их ранжирование в одной авторегрессивной модели на основе больших языковых моделей. Основная проблема заключается в том, что обратная связь по полезности приходит только после генерации всего отранжированного списка, что затрудняет определение, какой из двух этапов привёл к плохому результату. Авторы предлагают метод факторизованной групповой относительной оптимизации политики (F-GRPO), который разделяет политику на две части, но обучает их совместно с помощью отдельных наград за покрытие и позиционную полезность. Экспериментальные результаты на задачах последовательной рекомендации и многошаговых вопросо-ответных систем показывают, что такой подход улучшает качество ранжирования и конкурирует с сильными базовыми методами.

en.title: Unifying Candidate Generation and Ranking for Better Retrieval Optimization
en.desc: This paper presents a new approach to improve retrieval optimization by combining candidate generation and ranking into a single autoregressive model. It addresses the credit assignment problem, where it's difficult to determine if poor results come from generating irrelevant candidates or from incorrect ranking. The proposed method, called factorized group-relative policy optimization (F-GRPO), allows for joint training of generation and ranking using shared resources, which enhances performance. The results show that this unified framework outperforms traditional methods in various benchmarks, making it more efficient and effective for tasks like recommendation and question answering.

zh.title: 统一框架：候选生成与排序的完美结合
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种统一框架，将候选生成和排序结合在一个自回归模型中，利用分解的组相对策略优化来解决端到端检索优化中的信用分配问题。传统的检索流程将候选检索和重新排序分为两个阶段，而大型语言模型（LLM）则将其扩展为生成过程，能够在一次自回归传递中生成并排序候选子集。为了克服生成和排序之间的信用分配差距，本文提出的框架在共享同一LLM主干的基础上，分解了策略并通过无序覆盖奖励和位置感知效用奖励进行联合训练。实验结果表明，该框架在序列推荐和多跳问答基准测试中，显著提高了排名性能，且在推理时无需架构更改。

papers.title: WriteSAE: Sparse Autoencoders for Recurrent State
papers.abstract: Abstract WriteSAE enables sparse autoencoder decomposition and editing of matrix cache writes in state-space and hybrid recurrent language models, achieving superior performance in token-level interventions compared to existing methods.  AI-generated summary We introduce WriteSAE, the first sparse autoencoder that decomposes and edits the matrix cache write of state-space and hybrid recurrent language models, where residual SAEs cannot reach. Existing SAEs read residual streams, but Gated DeltaNet, Mamba-2, and RWKV-7 write to a d_k times d_v cache through rank-1 updates k_t v_t^top that no vector atom can replace. WriteSAE factors each decoder atom into the native write shape, exposes a closed form for the per-token logit shift, and trains under matched Frobenius norm so atoms swap one cache slot at a time. Atom substitution beats matched-norm ablation on 92.4% of n=4{,}851 firings at Qwen3.5-0.8B L9 H4, the 87-atom population test holds at 89.8%, the closed form predicts measured effects at R^2=0.98, and Mamba-2-370M substitutes at 88.1% over 2,500 firings. Sustained three-position installs at 3times lift midrank target-in-continuation from 33.3% to 100% under greedy decoding, the first behavioral install at the matrix-recurrent write site.

ru.title: Разреженные автоэнкодеры для декодирования и редактирования матричных кэшей в рекуррентных моделях
ru.desc: Работа представляет WriteSAE — первый разреженный автоэнкодер, который может разложить и редактировать операции записи в кэш матриц в моделях на основе state-space и гибридных рекуррентных архитектурах. В отличие от существующих SAE, которые работают с остаточными потоками, WriteSAE фактористирует каждый атом декодера в нативную форму записи и обеспечивает замкнутую форму для сдвига логитов на токене. Метод достигает высокой точности интервенций — замена атомов превосходит аблацию на 92.4% случаев, а закрытая форма предсказывает эффекты с R²=0.98. Это первый практический метод прямого редактирования на уровне матричной записи, позволяющий добиться значительного улучшения поведения модели при генерации.

en.title: Revolutionizing Token Interventions with WriteSAE
en.desc: The paper introduces WriteSAE, a novel sparse autoencoder designed for decomposing and editing matrix cache writes in state-space and hybrid recurrent language models. Unlike traditional sparse autoencoders that rely on residual streams, WriteSAE directly manipulates the matrix cache, allowing for more effective token-level interventions. The method demonstrates superior performance, achieving a high success rate in atom substitution and accurate predictions of logit shifts. Overall, WriteSAE represents a significant advancement in the manipulation of language model outputs, enhancing the efficiency of interventions in complex models.

zh.title: WriteSAE：提升语言模型的稀疏自编码器
zh.desc: 本文介绍了WriteSAE，这是一种稀疏自编码器，能够对状态空间和混合递归语言模型中的矩阵缓存写入进行分解和编辑。与现有方法相比，WriteSAE在令牌级干预中表现出更优的性能。它通过将每个解码器原子分解为原生写入形状，并在匹配的Frobenius范数下进行训练，从而实现了原子的逐个替换。实验结果显示，WriteSAE在多个测试中超越了传统方法，证明了其在语言模型中的有效性。

papers.title: SafeHarbor: Hierarchical Memory-Augmented Guardrail for LLM Agent Safety
papers.abstract: Abstract SafeHarbor is a novel framework for LLM agents that establishes precise decision boundaries through context-aware defense rules, featuring a hierarchical memory system and self-evolution mechanism to balance safety and utility.  AI-generated summary With the rapid evolution of foundation models, Large Language Model (LLM) agents have demonstrated increasingly powerful tool-use capabilities. However, this proficiency introduces significant security risks, as malicious actors can manipulate agents into executing tools to generate harmful content. While existing defensive mechanisms are effective, they frequently suffer from the over-refusal problem, where increased safety strictness compromises the agent's utility on benign tasks. To mitigate this trade-off, we propose SafeHarbor, a novel framework designed to establish precise decision boundaries for LLM agents. Unlike static guidelines, SafeHarbor extracts context-aware defense rules through enhanced adversarial generation. We design a local hierarchical memory system for dynamic rule injection, offering a training-free, efficient, and plug-and-play solution. Furthermore, we introduce an information entropy-based self-evolution mechanism that continuously optimizes the memory structure through dynamic node splitting and merging. Extensive experiments demonstrate that SafeHarbor achieves state-of-the-art performance on both ambiguous benign tasks and explicit malicious attacks, notably attaining a peak benign utility of 63.6\% on GPT-4o while maintaining a robust refusal rate exceeding 93\% against harmful requests. The source code is publicly available at https://github.com/ljj-cyber/SafeHarbor.

ru.title: Контекстная защита без потери функциональности
ru.desc: SafeHarbor — это фреймворк для защиты LLM агентов, который устанавливает точные границы принятия решений через контекстно-зависимые правила защиты. Система использует иерархическую память для динамического внедрения правил без необходимости переобучения модели. Фреймворк включает механизм самоэволюции на основе информационной энтропии, который непрерывно оптимизирует структуру памяти путём разбиения и объединения узлов. Подход решает проблему избыточного отказа, достигая высокой полезности на безопасных задачах (63.6% на GPT-4o) при сохранении надежного уровня отказа от вредоносных запросов (свыше 93%).

en.title: SafeHarbor: Balancing Safety and Utility in LLM Agents
en.desc: SafeHarbor is a new framework designed for Large Language Model (LLM) agents that aims to create clear decision boundaries using context-aware defense rules. It features a hierarchical memory system that allows for dynamic rule adjustments without the need for retraining, making it efficient and easy to implement. Additionally, SafeHarbor includes a self-evolution mechanism that optimizes its memory structure by adapting to new information, enhancing both safety and utility. The framework has shown impressive results, balancing high performance on benign tasks while maintaining strong defenses against malicious attacks.

zh.title: 安全与实用的平衡：SafeHarbor框架
zh.desc: SafeHarbor是一个新颖的框架，旨在为大型语言模型（LLM）代理建立精确的决策边界。它通过上下文感知的防御规则，结合分层记忆系统和自我进化机制，平衡安全性和实用性。与静态指导原则不同，SafeHarbor通过增强的对抗生成提取动态防御规则。实验表明，SafeHarbor在模糊的良性任务和明确的恶意攻击中均表现出色，良性实用性高达63.6%，同时对有害请求的拒绝率超过93%。
